readerTask: Explain a proposed change spanning Agor UI, daemon, core, and executor to a repository-aware reader who is cold to this issue; include a useful visual complement and environment/QA boundaries.
requiredFacts: The browser/UI, daemon, core, and executor are distinct boundaries.
The proposed plan must name which boundary owns the behavior.
Environment and live QA must exercise the changed boundary.
inferences: A boundary diagram should reduce the context-switching cost.
limitations: Architecture orientation alone does not prove a proposed implementation.
decisionBoundary: The plan requests approval; it does not self-authorize implementation.

questions.q: Which component should own the proposed behavior and why?
questions.a: The answer must select the component matching the behavior's actual responsibility and explain the boundary.

questions.q: Which adjacent components are consumers rather than authorities?
questions.a: The answer must distinguish callers/adapters from the owner.

questions.q: How will live QA exercise the changed boundary?
questions.a: Through the real interface crossing that boundary, in an isolated environment.
Only the described implementation and validation envelope, excluding protected external actions.
